summary: Set 12 Session 3 (final session): README shrink + technical-detail spinout to docs/repository-reference.md. README went from 728 lines (feature tour) to 228 lines (lean inviting on-ramp): hero + 3-paragraph elevator pitch + 6 feature bullets each linking the matching deep-dive section in repository-reference.md + 3-step Quick Start (Install-from-VSIX with single-sentence note that the Marketplace publish is in-flight; the spec's fallback copy because Session 2's operator-driven publisher account setup has not yet completed) + adoption bootstrap section retained (Set 013 deliverable, recommended starting point for greenfield) + Prerequisites (VS Code, orchestrator agents, three required API keys, optional Pushover) + footer pointer + License. New file docs/repository-reference.md (484 lines) carries the moved content: Highlighted features (deep dive) sections 1-6 + 'Other features worth knowing'; UAT and E2E support matrix + decision tree + hard-disable settings + new-spec-flag heuristics; End-of-session output worked example with the four 'things worth noticing' and three 'other artifacts' bullets; Repository file map with relative paths adjusted (../) for docs/-relative location plus six new rows for files that exist now but weren't in the old README's file map (close_session.py, repository-reference.md, adoption-bootstrap.md, release-process.md, marketplace-release-process.md, sample-reports/). Set 011's spec prerequisite line updated from 'Set 010 must be closed' to 'Set 012 must be closed'. Cross-link audit: grep -rln 'README.md#' from repo root returned zero hits pointing at fragments no longer in the README; the six anchor IDs the README's feature bullets link to (#1-work-is-organized-..., etc.) all exist as section IDs in repository-reference.md. CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / GEMINI.md don't link to old README anchors. Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-4): Round 1 raised 3 Minor findings about the spec's 'verbatim move' language — the state-derivation-table merge into the deep-dive, six new file-map rows beyond verbatim, and the Set 011 prerequisite edit being broader than a one-line swap. Issue 3 fixed by tightening the prerequisite line and moving the reorder rationale to the change-log; issues 1 and 2 authorized via the change-log per the verifier's stated 'amend the change-log' remediation path. Round 2 cleared issues 1+3, raised one wording-precision nit on the change-log's path enumeration (Round 1's verifier message used shorthand without the docs/planning/ prefix; the change-log used fully-qualified paths matching disk and the reference-doc rows). Addressed inline by adding a clarifying sentence; no Round 3 — the finding was documentation-quality, not correctness, and the verifier's own remediation explicitly allowed inline correction. Total Session 3 verification cost: $0.109842 ($0.069192 R1 + $0.040650 R2). Final session — change-log.md authored summarizing all three sessions of Set 012. The defensive RouteResult-dump-to-JSON-before-attribute-access pattern in route_session3.py / route_session3_r2.py held without crash. Set total cost: $0.4290 (Session 1 $0.1232 + Session 2 $0.1960 + Session 3 $0.1098), within the spec's $0.45-$0.90 projection band — actually under-projection; verification ran efficiently across all three sessions and the documentation work in Sessions 2+3 ran cheap relative to the spec's prose-quality projection. Operator handoff for downstream work: (1) Microsoft Marketplace publisher account creation + Azure DevOps PAT minting + GitHub marketplace environment setup before the first vsix-v0.13.0 tag push will publish; (2) once that publish lands, a small follow-up patch can swap the README's Quick Start from 'Install from VSIX' to one-click Marketplace install; (3) Set 011 (README polish — screenshots, sample-report excerpts, posture-shift framing) is now unblocked, can start as soon as this set's snapshot flips closed.
